name: West Virginia
examName: West Virginia Real Estate Salesperson Exam

examFacts.notes: Administered by Pearson VUE for the West Virginia Real Estate Commission.

intro:
West Virginia scores its national and state portions separately, so a strong national score won't rescue a weak state score. West Virginia layers its own license law on top of the national principles, and that West Virginia-specific material is exactly where unprepared candidates lose points.

West Virginia tests the duties and powers of the Real Estate Commission, the statutes that govern licensee conduct, the state's agency-disclosure rules, trust-account requirements, and West Virginia's own fair housing protections. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need West Virginia-specific practice questions.

faq.q: How many questions are on the West Virginia real estate exam?
faq.a: The West Virginia salesperson exam has 130 multiple-choice questions, split into an 80-question national portion and a 50-question state portion. You get 210 minutes in total, and you need 70% on each portion to pass.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the West Virginia real estate exam?
faq.a: You need 70% on each portion separately. The national and state sections are scored independently, so you have to clear both. You receive a score report marked pass or fail at the test center.

faq.q: How much does the West Virginia real estate exam cost?
faq.a: The examination fee is $59 per exam part, paid to Pearson VUE when you reserve your seat. Because the salesperson exam has a national part and a state part, plan for that fee on each part you need to take.

faq.q: How long is the West Virginia real estate exam?
faq.a: You get 150 minutes for the 80-question national portion and 60 minutes for the 50-question state portion, 210 minutes in all. That is a little over a minute per question, so a steady pace matters.

faq.q: Who administers the West Virginia real estate exam?
faq.a: The West Virginia Real Estate Salesperson Exam is administered by Pearson VUE for the West Virginia Real Estate Commission. You register and schedule your exam date through Pearson VUE.

faq.q: Is the West Virginia real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It is challenging, especially the state portion. Many candidates handle the national real estate principles fine but underestimate how much West Virginia-specific law the state section covers across its 50 questions. Focused state prep is what separates first-time passers from retakes.

faq.q: What's on the West Virginia real estate salesperson exam?
faq.a: The national portion covers property ownership, land use controls, valuation, financing, agency, property disclosures, contracts, leasing and property management, transfer of title, the practice of real estate, and real estate calculations. The state portion focuses on the duties and powers of the West Virginia Real Estate Commission, the statutes that govern licensee activity, agency-disclosure rules, broker trust-account requirements, and West Virginia fair housing law.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the West Virginia real estate exam?
faq.a: Practice with West Virginia-specific questions until you score above 80% consistently, then keep drilling the topics you miss most. Review every explanation, not just the ones you get wrong, and use practice tests that mirror the real exam's split national-and-state format and difficulty.

The 20 West Virginia practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the West Virginia bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the West Virginia real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated West Virginia question bank built for the West Virginia real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the West Virginia law that trips people up, like the duties and powers of the Real Estate Commission, the state's agency-disclosure rules, and West Virginia's own fair housing protections.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward West Virginia's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
